title: Wrongful Death Car Accident in Huntsville, Alabama — Family Guide

For Huntsville, Alabama families, Alabama's wrongful death statute provides a critical window to pursue justice, but that window is approximately 2 years from the date of loss. Missing that deadline can mean losing your right to compensation entirely.

reddit_title: Lost a family member in a car accident in Huntsville, AL — what are the wrongful death filing deadlines and what should we do first?
reddit_body:
First, I'm so sorry if you're dealing with this. Alabama's wrongful death statute generally gives families a 2-year window from the date of death to file a claim, but the exact deadline can vary based on circumstances — so please verify with a licensed Alabama attorney as soon as possible. Missing that window can permanently bar recovery, no matter how strong the case.

In the meantime, preserve everything you can: photos from the scene, witness contact information, the police report, medical records, and any communication with insurance companies. Do not give recorded statements to the at-fault driver's insurer without legal counsel present. Insurance adjusters work for the insurance company, not your family.

If you're struggling to find the right attorney, wreckmatch.com is a free matching service that connects accident victims and families with vetted personal injury and wrongful death lawyers. It won't cost you anything to get matched, and most wrongful death attorneys work on contingency — meaning no upfront fees. Take it one step at a time.
